info.description:
This is a sample documentation for TrekkSoft API integration. You can find out more about TrekkSoft at [https://support.trekksoft.com](https://support.trekksoft.com). # Introduction The following requirements must be satisfied for an API request to be successful: - Successful authentication using an API key - Use of `Accept` header with a supported Media Type - Use of `Accept-Language` header with a supported language - Use of correct HTTP verb - Existing resource identified by the URL path Please continue reading to get an in-depth explanation of all these requirements. # Authentication API v2 supports OAuth2 authentication to get access to the data. You will be provided with `client_id` and `client_secret` which you'll use to authenticate yourself in the system. OAuth2 - an open protocol to allow secure authorization in a simple and standard method from web, mobile and desktop applications. The first thing you need to do is to get access token which you will then use will all your requests. This token is valid for **1 hour only**. When it expires, you will need to request another one. You can get you access token by POSTing to the https://api2.trekksoft.com/oauth2/token endpoint the following parameters: - grant_type (this should always be "client_credentials") - client_id - client_secret 
curl --data \
"grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=username&client_secret=password" \
https://api2.trekksoft.com/oauth2/token
Example Response: 
{
    "access_token": "79ab80aba81592906b43a3d10d7c49c68cf0951a",
    "expires_in": 3600,
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "scope": null
}
# Authorization of requests Once you have a valid access token, you will need to use it to sign all requests made to the TrekkSoft API. This is done by providing `Authorization` HTTP header along with each of your requests in the following format: `Authorization: ` Example Request: 
curl -k -H"Authorization: Bearer 79ab80aba81592906b43a3d10d7c49c68cf0951a" \
https://api2.trekksoft.com/activities?limit=1
# Extras ## Media Types Custom media types are used in the API to allow developers choose the format and version of the data they wish to receive. This is done by adding one or more of the following types to the `Accept` header when sending a request. 
application/vnd.trekksoft[.version][+format]
application/json
As of v1 of the API the following `Accept` header is recommended: 
application/vnd.trekksoft.v1+json, application/json; q=.5
## Schema All requests to the TrekkSoft API must be over HTTPS using the domain `api2.trekksoft.com`. Example Request: 
curl -i https://api2.trekksoft.com/
Example Response (to an unauthorized request): 
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Mon, 10 Apr 2015 10:26:20 GMT

{}
## HTTP Verbs The API makes use of the HTTP verbs to indicate what kind of request you are sending. 
GET    /activities        Lists all resources of the 'activities' collection.
POST   /activities        Creates a new resource in the 'activities' collection.
PUT    /activities        Updates multiple resources in the 'activities' collection.
DELETE /activities        Deletes multiple resources in the 'activities' collection.
GET    /activities/435    Reads a resource from the 'activities' collection.
PUT    /activities/435    Updates a resource in the 'activities' collection.
DELETE /activities/435    Deletes a resource in the 'activities' collection.
Please note, that not every collection supports all HTTP verbs. ## Time Periods Some date fields support additional time period notation parameters. The following syntax is supported: 
[PERIOD_START][,PERIOD_END]

2014-01-01T00:00:00+02:00                            # After/at 2014-01-01 00:00:00
2014-01-01T00:00:00+02:00,2014-01-31T23:59:59+02:00  # Between 2014-01-01 00:00:00
                                                     # and 2014-01-31 23:59:59
2014-01-31T23:59:59+02:00
## Pagination Requests that return multiple items are paginated to 30 items by default. Developers can control pagination by using the `offset` (default: `0`) and `limit` (default: `30`) query parameters. The custom limit may not exceed `100`. 
GET /activities?offset=0&limit=20
GET /activities?offset=20&limit=20
GET /activities?offset=40&limit=20

get.description:
Gets a list of activities information.

⚠️ **Preview endpoint**

This endpoint represents the upcoming **v2 response format** and is currently
provided for testing and migration purposes. Once released, it will replace
the current `/activities` endpoint.

**Changes introduced in V2**

- The `_links` property of the `Activity` object has been extended.
- A new property `_links.locales` has been added.
- `_links.locales.documents` contains document links grouped by locale code.
- This allows clients to access informational documents for languages other than the default activity language.

parameters.description: Filter for activity IDs. Could be one (or several) IDs

parameters.description: Filter for merchant slug code. Could be one (or several) merchants

parameters.name: category

parameters.name: departureCountryCode
parameters.description: Filter for activity departure country. Could be one (or several) codes

parameters.description: Filter for activity departure city. Could be one (or several) codes

get.description:
Gets information for the specific activity

⚠️ **Preview endpoint**

This endpoint represents the upcoming **v2 response format** and is currently
provided for testing and migration purposes. Once released, it will replace
the current `/activities/{activityId}` endpoint.

**Changes introduced in V2**

- The `_links` property of the `Activity` object has been extended.
- A new property `_links.locales` has been added.
- `_links.locales.documents` contains document links grouped by locale code.
- This allows clients to access informational documents for languages other than the default activity language.

parameters.name: bookedAfter
parameters.description: Filters bookings by the booking date and time. _Sorting from last booked booking to first._

parameters.name: cancelledAfter
parameters.description:
Filters bookings by the last cancellation date. _Sorting from last cancellation to first._ _If `cancelledAfter` is used in combination with `changedAfter`,
  it will filter by `updatedAt` and `cancelledAt` fields.
  But sorting will be done by `updatedAt` field only._

get.description: Requests which availability rules are available

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter for availability rule IDs. Could be one (or several) IDs. Multiple filters are possible `id[]=6`

parameters.name: activityId
parameters.description: Filter for activity IDs. Could be one (or several) IDs. Multiple filters are possible `activityId[]=506`

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Availability types to filter for. Should be one (or several) of `trip`, `day_trip`, `rental`, `voucher` Example `type[]=day_trip`

parameters.name: merchant
parameters.description: Filter for merchant code. Could be one merchant

parameters.name: isActive
parameters.description: Can be `0` or `1`. `0` - only inactive availability rules, `1` - active only. If parameter doesn't set - all rules fetches

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset

example.error_description: Invalid request parameters

errors.[id]: This value should be type of array.

example.error_description: The access token provided is invalid

parameters.name: activityId
parameters.description: Filter for activity IDs. Could be one (or several) IDs. Multiple filters are possible `activityId[]=5`

parameters.name: titleLike
parameters.description: Filter by activity title. Could be a part of the full title

parameters.name: availabilityRuleId
parameters.description: Availability rule id to filter by

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Availability types to filter for. Should be one (or several) `type[]=trip`

parameters.name: bookingTime
parameters.description: Date/time in ISO 8601 format of when you expect booking to happen (usually you should set it to the current date and time). It would filter out availability items which are not bookable after this time.                     It is necessary to use [URL encoded](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/ref_urlencode.ASP) value eg. `2021-04-16T07:27:57+0000` value should be passed as `2021-04-16T07%3A27%3A57%2B0000`

parameters.name: seats
parameters.description: Quantity of required seats (defaults to 1). Providing this number would filter out availability items which don't have enough remaining seats for the booking. Bear in mind that this only applies generic filter for availability items, individual limits per price categories might apply. If your oauth key allows overbooking for certain merchants, this filter would be ignored

parameters.name: activeOnly
parameters.description: Set this to false to get items for all availability items (including disabled/blocked ones)

parameters.name: withExclusive
parameters.description: Set this to true to include exclusive private events in the result

parameters.name: withCustom
parameters.description: Set this to false to exclude custom trips (default true)

parameters.name: onlyBooked
parameters.description: Return availabilities that were booked at least once

parameters.name: onlyNotBooked
parameters.description: Return availabilities that never booked

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Price categories to search for in format (id => available seats) `category[123]=5`

parameters.name: merchant
parameters.description: Filter for merchant code. Could be one (or several) merchants. Multiple filters are possible `merchant[]=demo`

parameters.name: startsFrom
parameters.description: Activity start datetime range begin

parameters.name: startsTo
parameters.description: Activity start date range end

parameters.name: departureTime
parameters.description: Departure time

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Filter for availability language. If not provided, availability details in merchant's default language will be returned

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset

example.error_description: Invalid request parameters

errors.[activityId]: This value should be type of array.

example.error_description: The access token provided is invalid

post.description:
Captures (reserves) specific availability for future booking. Usually done on the checkout page just before placing a booking.
*Important note:* make sure you take into consideration `minSeats` and `maxSeats` information for availability.
E.g. if you want to book 3 single seat items (maxSeats = 1) for price category 567, you have to treat them as separate: `[{567:1}, {567:1}, {567:1}]`
For group seats (minSeats > 1) you need to specify quantity directly: `[{765: 3}]`
If minSeats, maxSeats or available seats limitations are exceeded, response with status `400` will be returned (with error details) and items will not be captured.

example.error_description: Invalid request parameters

errors.[availabilityItemId]: This value should be type of string.

example.error_description: The access token provided is invalid

delete.description: Uncaptures (releases) previously captured availability. This is usually needed when basket is cancelled to free up capacity for other users

parameters.description: Capture reference received when availability was captured

availabilityRuleId.description: Rule id of the item change was applied to

priceCategoryId.description: Id of the price category (if any) the change was applied to. `NULL` is returned if change is applied to the item it general

get.description: Returns a list of all navigation groups that are used on the mobile version of the website

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: Unique numeric identifier of a merchant

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: One of the following - `sequenceIndex` (By the defined sequence indexes) or `near` (By city closest to the `position` parameter)

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Used with the order type near. Must be string separating a latitude and longitude with a comma

id.description: Unique numeric identifier of the tour group

city.description: City associated with the tour group

country_code.description: The 2-digit ISO country code of the tour group

title.description: The title of the tour group

get.description: Validate ticket code. If ticket code is valid then corresponding guest will be checked-in automatically.

parameters.description: Ticket code to validate

error.example: Cannot find this ticket in the system

errors.[code]: This value should not be blank.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Ticket code to validate

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: For "guest" scope this method behaves similar to "Validate ticket" method. For "booking" scope and a single guest in a booking this method behaves similar to "Validate ticket" so the guest will be checked-in automatically. For "booking" scope and multiple guests in a booking this method doesn't do check-in for any guest but just returns info regarding a guest related to the ticket and also returns a list of other guests from the same booking.

locales.description:
Locale specific documents.  
Key = locale code (e.g. `de`, `en`, `en_AU`, `es`).  
Value = list of document URLs for that locale.

documents.description:
Map where the key(`property name`) is the locale code (e.g. `de`, `en`, `en_AU`, `es`, etc.)
and the value is a list of document URLs for that locale.

additionalProperties.description: List of document URLs for the given locale.

fullCaption.example: Actividad Amigo - Toledo Express Tour From Madrid (3/27/2018 7:45 AM)

title.description: Actividad Amigo - Toledo Express Tour From Madrid

isCancelled.description: Is booking item cancelled of not

refId.description: Booking Shop Item id (references with Booking)

title.example: 1 Person Deposit Voucher for Inca Trail 4 Day Expedition activity

isCancelled.description: Is Shop Item cancelled or not

isActive.description: Is item active and can be bought or not

description.description: Shop Item description in the current language (contains HTML markup)

voucherAmount.description: If shop item is a `certificate` the object will have same structure as price (including price and currency) otherwise null

expirationDate.description: If type is `certificate` the value will show latest date when the certificate which was bought can be redeemed. Can be null

images.description: Shop Item images with different sizes. If images are not uploaded - will be an empty array

restrictions.description: Certificate restrictions. Null for non-certificate items. If restrictions are not provided - certificate can be used for all inventory.

activities.description: If it contains at least one selection then the certificate's vouchers will remain usable for ALL activities UNLESS it's limited to any addons and/or shop items

addons.description: If it contains at least one selection then the certificate's vouchers will remain usable for ALL addons UNLESS it's limited to any activities and/or shop items

shopItems.description: If it contains at least one selection then the certificate's vouchers will remain usable for ALL shop items UNLESS it's limited to any activities and/or addons

address1.description: First line of the merchant's address

address2.description: Second line of the merchant's address
address2.example: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, q

zip.description: ZIP code of the merchant's address

supportedLanguages.description: A list of languages supported by the merchant

userFields.description: A list of extra details which users are required to fill in when making a booking with this merchant

defaultCurrency.description: Default currency of the merchant in ISO4217 format

currencies.description: Currencies which are supported by merchant in ISO4217 format (see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217 for more info)

paymentGateways.description: List of payment gateways current merchant supports

partnerCurrencies.description: Currencies which are supported by merchant's partners in ISO4217 format (see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217 for more info)

conversionRates.description: Conversion rates for this merchant from default currency

formats.description: Various display formats specific to merchant (and his language!). For more info on date/time formats see http://php.net/manual/en/function.date.php

multiDiscounts.description: Possible multi-discounts supported by this merchant

multiDiscountByItems.description: Multi-discount percentage depending from number of unique items

subTotal.description: Booking sub total (without taxes and fees)

fullCaption.example: 1 x Actividad Amigo - Toledo Express Tour From Madrid

balance.description: Return the balance of this booking. The value is negative if some money is outstanding, zero when the balance is even and positive if the user overpaid or we owe him money.

createdAt.description: Date and time of booking creation

updatedAt.description: Date and time of the last booking update

bookedAt.description: Date and time of booking completion

cancelledAt.description: Date and time of the last booking cancellation (for fully cancelled bookings only)

items.description:
Items list, details are explained below.
NB. You will only see booking items as long as you’ve sold the booking or you’re carrying out the trip/activity.

shopItemsPrice.description: Total price for all shop items

vouchers.description: A list of vouchers codes along with discount amount for each voucher

included.description: Indicates whether the tax is included into the price or not

value.description: Tax value, structure depends on tax (can be `percent` or `fixed`)

amount.description: Tax amount (how much money it is)

customValues.description: Extra details for the user, different depending on merchant

commission.description: Agent's commission. Will show "custom" when Commission per Product feature is enabled indicating that the more detailed "commissions" field should be used instead.

commissions.description: Agent's Custom Commissions. Only shown when Commission per Product feature is enabled.

isReservation.description: A booking is considered a reservation if the status is PAID, but no payments were added

isConfirmationRequired.description: A booking require confirmation if the status is BOOKED and booking was made with activity which have isConfirmation enabled

hasRefundProtect.description: A booking is protected when at least one payment is protected by refund protect

hasOnlineBookingFee.description: Indicates whether an online booking fee has been applied to the booking or not. If it has been applied and has been canceled, it will return false

itemDuration.description: Duration of each item in the rule

cutOffTime.description: The number of minutes before you stop accepting bookings for a trip. Example: If your trip starts at 3pm and your cut-off time is at 2pm, type in 60

tag.description: Special tag for schedule. Commonly, used for filtering in reports

latestBookingTime.description: Latest time when a booking is allowed. After this time a booking will not be possible if you don't have permissions to book past trips

categories.description: A list of all booking options

availableSeats.description: Quantity of available seats for price category (price categories with no available seats are not returned). Might return NULL if there is no limit for price category (in this case global available seats limit is applied, see availableSeats option above)

capacity.description: Capacity of availability for price category. Might return NULL if there is no limit for price category (in this case global capacity limit is applied, see capacity option above)

occupancy.description: Occupancy of availability for price category

minSeats.description: Minimum number of seats which is allowed for this price category (used for group bookings)

maxSeats.description: Maximum number of seats which is allowed for this price category

isCustom.description: Whether this category is custom or not

availableSeats.description: Quantity of available seats for availability. Note that individual limits for each category might be applied (see categories section below)

capacity.description: Capacity of availability (e.g. how many people can the boat contain). Note that individual limits for each category might be applied (see categories section below)

occupancy.description: Occupancy of availability (how many seats are reserved)

occupancyStatus.description: Available seats status. Can be `inactive`, `resource-full`, `full`, `almostfull` (more than 80% booked), `bookedsome` (booked at least 1), `free`

isExclusive.description: Whether this availability is exclusive or not

isActive.description: Whether this availability is active or not

isParent.description: Whether this availability is parent for other or not

itemDuration.description: Duration of each item in the rental

cutOffTime.description: The number of minutes before you stop accepting bookings for a trip. Example: If your trip starts at 3pm and your cut-off time is at 2pm, type in 60

tag.description: Special tag for schedule. Commonly, used for filtering in reports

title.description: Package's title in the current language

description.description: Package's description in the current language (contains HTML markup)
description.example: Guided by local experts you experience and learn about the beauty and history of Interlaken and its surroundings.

itinerary.description: Package's itinerary description in the current language (contains HTML markup)

Tours run daily
Rain or Shine / whatever the weather :)

addons.description: A list of addons available for this package

fromPrice.description: Starting price for this package which is set up by merchant (for reference, actual price might differ depending on selected price categories and discounts during booking process). Might be `NULL` if there's nothing to sell or no price specified

route.description: A list of route points available for this package

XS.description: Package header image with max size 150x100

S.description: Package header image with max size 300x200

M.description: Package header image with max size 600x400

L.description: Package header image with max size 900x600

XL.description: Package header image with max size 1200x800

Gallery image with max size 2000x2000 (can be 1500x900 or 1480x920 for example - it depends on width and height of the original image)

options.description: A list of available booking options for this package

originalPrice.description: Original activities price (sum) in the option

availabilityCaptureReference.description: Availability capture reference which should have previously been received at *Capture Availability* call. When booking is created, this reference will no longer be valid

availabilityItemId.description: One or group of Availability Item ID's (separated with comma) which need to be booked

occupancy.description: How many seats to occupy for guest

addons.description: Ids of addons applied for this guest

remarks.example: This is the best guest ever

customFields.description: A list of guest's custom field values in format id: value

addons.example: Addons applied for this availability item: id and quantity

customFields.description: A list of bookee's custom field values

type.description: Contact type. You can provide correct email or mobile phone. Also you can set alternative string or empty string if basket booked is not contactable

notify.description: Flag specifying whether notification should be sent on this contact channel or not. True by default. The setting is ignored for online paid bookings. Notification is sent once payment is done. Currently only email notifications are supported

multidiscount.description: Custom multidiscount percentage (only for agent bookings). Overrides default multidiscount (if there was any)

payment.description: Offline payment details (only for agent bookings). Omit it for creating a reservation

amount.description: Amount which user paid to the agent

id.description: ID of the shop item which has to be booked

quantity.description: Number of shop items to be booked

number.description: Custom booking number. This parameter is only displayed for marketplace bookings and not for custom API integrations

agentCode.description: Agent’s code in the format agent-agentId where agentId is the ID of an existing agent. (Example: agent-12345)

creditCard.description: Credit card alias info. Pass this information if you are paying using [Payyo Vault](https://developers.payyo.ch/guides/vault).

returnUrl.description: In case of 3d secure this url will be used for callback

payworks.description: Payworks transaction info. Pass this information if you are paying by Payworks

trekkPayPSP.description: TrekkPay PSP integration. Add this information if you would like your guest to pay via TrekkPay. If booking is possible, a booking in “paying” state will be created. Then, a `redirectUrl` and `id` will be returned. The guest has to be redirected to the URL to finish the payment. If payment is not finished within 15 minutes, the booking will be deleted and the user will have to restart the booking process.

successUrl.description: The guest will be redirected to this URL when payment is successful. Make sure to have a valid URL.

cancelUrl.description:
The guest will be redirected back to this URL if payment is not completed for any of the following reasons: user interrupted the payment, failed in 3d secure or there was any error.
If the guest is redirected to this URL, the booking will be deleted automatically. Make sure to have a valid URL.

language.description: The payment page can be set up in these languages: en, de, es and it. If your chose language isn’t supported, the English version will be shown.

confirmAndChargeLater.description: Use "Confirm and Charge Later" flow or not. If true then credit card or TrekkPay PSP integration settings should be defined

note.description: Whatever note you want to add to the booking

recipient.description: Email address to send tickets to

BookingTicketRequest.description: Send all tickets of booking to the given email

treasurerType.description: Who is receiving or received the payment

returnUrl.description: In case of 3d secure this url will be used for callback

BookingCardAliasPaymentRequest.description: Adds credit card payment to a booking

transactionId.description: ID of transaction which is going to be refunded. Required when `refundType` is `trekksoft`

BookingNumberRequest.description: Set a custom number to a booking. This parameter is only displayed for marketplace bookings and not for custom API integrations

reason.description: Comment for the reason of cancellation

refundFee.description: Refund online booking fee after canceling this item

reason.description: Comment for the reason of cancellation

itemId.description: Id of the item to be booked

ruleId.description: Rule id of the item, together with itemId it uniquely identifies an item

priceCategoryId.description: Price category id to be rebooked into. If price category id is not specified current price category of the guest will be used

price.description: If price is not specified price of the price category will be used

seats.description: Number of seats. If seats are not specified number of seats from the guest will be used

currency.description: Currency which should be used for calculation

items.description: Items for which price should be calculated. Details are explained below

occupancy.description: How many seats to occupy for guest

addons.description: Addons applied for this availability item

multidiscount.description: Custom multidiscount percentage (only for agent bookings). Overrides default multidiscount (if there was any)

defaultPrice.description: Default reselling price. Set defaultPrice to "null" to reset default reselling price

price.description: Reselling price. Set price to "null" to reset reselling price for particular reseller

reason.description: Comment for the reason of cancellation
Comment for the reason of cancellation

custom-field-id.description: Add custom fields values to request in format `custom-field-id`: value to set up custom values. This values could be required if custom field was flagged as mandatory.

GuestEditRequest.description: Updates guest information. You can update guest information only if basket is not completed.

itemId.description: Id of the item to be booked

ruleId.description: Rule id of the item, together with itemId it uniquely identifies an item

priceCategoryId.description: Price category id to be rebooked into. If price category id is not specified current price category of the guest will be used

price.description: If price is not specified price of the price category will be used

seats.description: Number of seats. If seats are not specified number of seats from the guest will be used

priceCategories.description: Price categories to be captured in `[{"id":"quantity"},...]` format. Alternatively `{"id":"quantity", ...}` format can be used.

duration.description: Specifies for how long availability should be captured. Set this to the minimal required duration you require so that not to block bookings for other users. Note that server might limit capture duration time in case you request too long duration. Duration is set in ISO 8601 format

reference.description: Use it for re-capturing. If reference is provided, it will be uncaptured first and a new capture is created with details provided in this request

capacity.description: Capacity which is to be set for item or item price category

ruleId.description: Rule id of the item, together with itemId it uniquely identifies an item

categoryId.description: Price category id for which capacity needs to be set. If omitted, capacity set for item in general

grant_type.description: For all requests it should be `client_credentials`

client_secret.description: Unique secret key. Please, do not provide it to no one
